visa_types.name: Re-entry/TBT Application
visa_types.documents_required: Please click on the link Re-entry/TBT Application
visa_types.visa_fees:
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
- VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19
visa_types.processing_time: Please refer to the Danish Immigration Service homepage  (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/)to obtain information about the processing time for an application of residence permit.
visa_types.forms: There are different applications forms dependent on which type of residence permit you apply for. You can see the list of forms on this link (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/NR/rdonlyres/AEBBEB05-947E-4B33-81E9-98D34CC12E48/0/us_form_guide_eng.pdf) and download the relevant form for your application.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A residence permit is given only upon approval by the Danish Immigration Service in Copenhagen – the process may take 4-6 months. Such a permit will be valid for Denmark only, not the entire Schengen-area. Application forms can be downloaded here. (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/NR/rdonlyres/AEBBEB05-947E-4B33-81E9-98D34CC12E48/0/us_form_guide_eng.pdf) (Please note that each of the Immigration Service’s application forms for Danish residence and work permits have been developed for a specific type of applicant.)
Further information can be found at the website of the Danish Immigration Service. (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A residence permit is given only upon approval by the Danish Immigration Service in Copenhagen – the process may take 4-6 months. Such a permit will be valid for Denmark only, not the entire Schengen-area. Application forms can be downloaded here. (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/NR/rdonlyres/AEBBEB05-947E-4B33-81E9-98D34CC12E48/0/us_form_guide_eng.pdf) (Please note that each of the Immigration Service’s application forms for Danish residence and work permits have been developed for a specific type of applicant.)
Further information can be found at the website of the Danish Immigration Service. (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/)

## Visa Fees
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
- VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19

## Documents Required

Please click on the link Re-entry/TBT Application

## Photo Specifications
Applicants must not be asked to submit a physical passport photograph in cases where biometrics are captured (including digital photo enrolment) at the VAC.

## Processing Time
Please refer to the Danish Immigration Service homepage  (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/)to obtain information about the processing time for an application of residence permit.

## Download Forms
There are different applications forms dependent on which type of residence permit you apply for. You can see the list of forms on this link (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/NR/rdonlyres/AEBBEB05-947E-4B33-81E9-98D34CC12E48/0/us_form_guide_eng.pdf) and download the relevant form for your application.

visa_types.name: Business/Conference
visa_types.documents_required:
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
visa_types.visa_fees:
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19
visa_types.processing_time:
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 calendar days. This period may be extended
                                                                        up to a maximum of 60 calendar days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside
                                                                        the Schengen area and back again during your visit. If you apply for two or several entries, this must be justified.
visa_types.forms:
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa
                                                                        fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’
                                                                        .
                                                                    
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form 
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you can walk into the Centre to submit your documents without an appointment between 0800hrs and 1200hrs. You must book an appointment if you want to submit your documents between 1200hrs and 1500hrs at VFS by clicking here. (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/login) Make sure to bring along all the required documents (#) and hand
                                                                        in supporting documents at the Visa Application  Centre (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/attend-centre/Nairobi).
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to visit Denmark for Business / Conference , please check the requirements in the next page.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to visit Denmark for Business / Conference , please check the requirements in the next page.

## Visa Fees
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants must not be asked to submit a physical passport photograph in cases where biometrics are captured (including digital photo enrolment) at the VAC.

## Processing Time
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 calendar days. This period may be extended
                                                                        up to a maximum of 60 calendar days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside
                                                                        the Schengen area and back again during your visit. If you apply for two or several entries, this must be justified. 

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa
                                                                        fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’
                                                                        .
                                                                    
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form 
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you can walk into the Centre to submit your documents without an appointment between 0800hrs and 1200hrs. You must book an appointment if you want to submit your documents between 1200hrs and 1500hrs at VFS by clicking here. (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/login) Make sure to bring along all the required documents (#) and hand
                                                                        in supporting documents at the Visa Application  Centre (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/attend-centre/Nairobi).
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to travel to Denmark for studies or research, please follow these instructions.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to travel to Denmark for studies or research, please follow these instructions.

## Visa Fees
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
- VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants must not be asked to submit a physical passport photograph in cases where biometrics are captured (including digital photo enrolment) at the VAC.

## Processing Time
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 calendar days. This period may be extended
                                                                        up to a maximum of 60 calendar days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside
                                                                        the Schengen area and back again during your visit. If you apply for two or several entries, this must be justified. For more information on processing time please Click here (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/processing_time.htm).

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa
                                                                        fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’
                                                                        .
                                                                    
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form 
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you can walk into the Centre to submit your documents without an appointment between 0800hrs and 1200hrs. You must book an appointment if you want to submit your documents between 1200hrs and 1500hrs at VFS by clicking here. (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/login) Make sure to bring along all the required documents (#) and hand
                                                                        in supporting documents at the Visa Application  Centre (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/attend-centre/Nairobi).
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.

visa_types.name: Short Stay Visa
visa_types.overview:
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
See the list of countries whose citizens need a visa to enter Denmark (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/need_visa/who_needs_visa.htm).
The purpose of applying a visa requirement to citizens of certain countries is to control who can enter and visit Denmark and the other Schengen countries. The Schengen countries normally decide cooperatively which nationalities the visa requirement will be applied to. The countries are selected with consideration to immigration and security issues, as well as political concerns.
Kindly be informed the list of countries - whose nationals are subject to prior consultation in connection with visa applications - has been updated as of 1.12.2017. Kenya has now been included in the list – other countries in the region, from where the Danish Embassy, Nairobi receives applications, are: DRC, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Nigeria, Rwanda, Somalia, South Sudan and Sudan. Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/denmark/pdf/schengen-consultation_d14da71ba89d98784907c4852aa80f2e0fe97e031d0085d34053d96cc6bf3d3e.pdf) to download the complete list.
Please note a consultation in the Schengen visa system takes an average of 8 days from the moment the Embassy has received the application and started the processing, and it is not possible to issue a visa, before the consultation has been finalized. Expedited processing and derogation from the consultation process is not possible.
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Denmark and/or the other Schengen countries for a limited period of time. If you wish to reside in Denmark for an extended period of time, you need to apply for a residence permit. If the immigration authorities suspect that you intend to seek permanent or long-term residency in Denmark or another Schengen country without legal grounds, your visa application will be turned down. This applies for instance if you are applying for a residence permit and visa at the same time, or if you have a residence permit application pending with the Immigration Service.
If you have been granted certain types of residence or re-entry permits in another Schengen country, you do not need a visa to enter Denmark. Read more about residence permits issued by another Schengen country (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/schengen/residence_permit_schengen.htm). These types of residence permits are not valid for entry into the Faroe Islands or Greenland.
If you hold certain types of residence permits issued by Bulgaria, Cyprus or Rumania you are permitted to travel through Denmark to the country that has issued the residence permit, but you may not stop over without reason. Your journey through Denmark may last no longer than five days.
You do not need a visa to enter Denmark if you hold a residence card issued under the EU regulations on free movement, and your residence card was issued by an EU country which is also a Schengen country. If you hold an EU residence card issued by an EU country which is not a Schengen country, you can enter Denmark without a visa only if you are accompanied by, or will join, an EU citizen. This applies to both residence cards issued in accordance with Directive 2004/38/EC and residence cards issued before this directive took effect. You will receive your residence card in the form of a plastic card the size of a credit card or a residence sticker placed in your passport.
Family members of an EU/EEA citizen or Swiss citizen who is exercising his/her right to free movement in Denmark, as well as family members of a Danish citizen who is exercising or who has exercised his/her right to free movement to relocate to another EU/EEA country or Switzerland, have the right to have a visa application processed in accordance with EU regulations. Read more about visas issued under EU regulations (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/visas-under-eu-regulations.htm).
visa_types.full_text:
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
See the list of countries whose citizens need a visa to enter Denmark (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/need_visa/who_needs_visa.htm).
The purpose of applying a visa requirement to citizens of certain countries is to control who can enter and visit Denmark and the other Schengen countries. The Schengen countries normally decide cooperatively which nationalities the visa requirement will be applied to. The countries are selected with consideration to immigration and security issues, as well as political concerns.
Kindly be informed the list of countries - whose nationals are subject to prior consultation in connection with visa applications - has been updated as of 1.12.2017. Kenya has now been included in the list – other countries in the region, from where the Danish Embassy, Nairobi receives applications, are: DRC, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Nigeria, Rwanda, Somalia, South Sudan and Sudan. Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/denmark/pdf/schengen-consultation_d14da71ba89d98784907c4852aa80f2e0fe97e031d0085d34053d96cc6bf3d3e.pdf) to download the complete list.
Please note a consultation in the Schengen visa system takes an average of 8 days from the moment the Embassy has received the application and started the processing, and it is not possible to issue a visa, before the consultation has been finalized. Expedited processing and derogation from the consultation process is not possible.
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Denmark and/or the other Schengen countries for a limited period of time. If you wish to reside in Denmark for an extended period of time, you need to apply for a residence permit. If the immigration authorities suspect that you intend to seek permanent or long-term residency in Denmark or another Schengen country without legal grounds, your visa application will be turned down. This applies for instance if you are applying for a residence permit and visa at the same time, or if you have a residence permit application pending with the Immigration Service.
If you have been granted certain types of residence or re-entry permits in another Schengen country, you do not need a visa to enter Denmark. Read more about residence permits issued by another Schengen country (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/schengen/residence_permit_schengen.htm). These types of residence permits are not valid for entry into the Faroe Islands or Greenland.
If you hold certain types of residence permits issued by Bulgaria, Cyprus or Rumania you are permitted to travel through Denmark to the country that has issued the residence permit, but you may not stop over without reason. Your journey through Denmark may last no longer than five days.
You do not need a visa to enter Denmark if you hold a residence card issued under the EU regulations on free movement, and your residence card was issued by an EU country which is also a Schengen country. If you hold an EU residence card issued by an EU country which is not a Schengen country, you can enter Denmark without a visa only if you are accompanied by, or will join, an EU citizen. This applies to both residence cards issued in accordance with Directive 2004/38/EC and residence cards issued before this directive took effect. You will receive your residence card in the form of a plastic card the size of a credit card or a residence sticker placed in your passport.
Family members of an EU/EEA citizen or Swiss citizen who is exercising his/her right to free movement in Denmark, as well as family members of a Danish citizen who is exercising or who has exercised his/her right to free movement to relocate to another EU/EEA country or Switzerland, have the right to have a visa application processed in accordance with EU regulations. Read more about visas issued under EU regulations (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/visas-under-eu-regulations.htm).

## What does a visa entitle you to?
A visa normally grants you the right to stay in the entire Schengen region.
A visa grants you the right to spend a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period in the Schengen region.
Read more about duration here (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/duration/how_long.htm)
If you remain in Denmark after your visa expires or if you attempt to use your visa stay to obtain permanent or long-term residency in Denmark, you can be given a penalty period of three or five years. During the penalty period you will not be able to obtain a visa to visit Denmark. Read more about misuse of a visa (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/when_is_visa_granted/misuse-of-a-visa.htm).
A visa does not allow you to work in Denmark unless the Immigration Service has explicitly granted you this right.
However, during visits of less than 90 days you may carry out certain work-related activities without holding a work permit. Read more about visa and work permit (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/when_is_visa_granted/visa-or-work-permit.htm).
Read more about the general conditions for granting a visa (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/when_is_visa_granted/when_is_visa_granted.htm) and read more about how to apply for visa (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/how-to/).
Read the latest passport and visa promulgation from The Ministry of Justice (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/passport_and_visa_legislation.htm).

visa_types.name: Seafarer
visa_types.documents_required:
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
visa_types.visa_fees:
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
- VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19
visa_types.processing_time: According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 calendar days. This period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside the Schengen area and back again during your visit. If you apply for two or several entries, this must be justified.
visa_types.forms:
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you can walk into the Centre to submit your documents without an appointment between 0800hrs and 1200hrs. You must book an appointment if you want to submit your documents between 1200hrs and 1500hrs at VFS by clicking here. (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/login) Make sure to bring along all the required documents (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/denmark/Kenya/english/#) and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/attend-centre/Nairobi).
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
- If you wish to visit Denmark if you wish to join a ship that docks in Denmark.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- If you wish to visit Denmark if you wish to join a ship that docks in Denmark.

## Visa Fees
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
- VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants must not be asked to submit a physical passport photograph in cases where biometrics are captured (including digital photo enrolment) at the VAC.

## Processing Time
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 calendar days. This period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside the Schengen area and back again during your visit. If you apply for two or several entries, this must be justified.

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you can walk into the Centre to submit your documents without an appointment between 0800hrs and 1200hrs. You must book an appointment if you want to submit your documents between 1200hrs and 1500hrs at VFS by clicking here. (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/login) Make sure to bring along all the required documents (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/denmark/Kenya/english/#) and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/attend-centre/Nairobi).
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to travel to Denmark to participate in a cultural or sport event please follow these instructions.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to travel to Denmark to participate in a cultural or sport event please follow these instructions.

## Visa Fees
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants must not be asked to submit a physical passport photograph in cases where biometrics are captured (including digital photo enrolment) at the VAC.

## Processing Time
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 calendar days. This period may be extended
                                                                        up to a maximum of 60 calendar days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside
                                                                        the Schengen area and back again during your visit. If you apply for two or several entries, this must be justified. visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
See the list of countries whose citizens need a visa to enter Denmark.  Click Do I need a visa (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/need_visa/who_needs_visa.htm)?
Click  here (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/visa.htm) for General Information on Schengen Short-Term Visas
IMPORTANT NOTE – All applicants are requested to bring color photocopies of all passport pages (Please do not bring copies of blank pages) and all supporting documents in a standard single
                                                                        format (A4)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
See the list of countries whose citizens need a visa to enter Denmark.  Click Do I need a visa (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/need_visa/who_needs_visa.htm)?
Click  here (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/visa.htm) for General Information on Schengen Short-Term Visas
IMPORTANT NOTE – All applicants are requested to bring color photocopies of all passport pages (Please do not bring copies of blank pages) and all supporting documents in a standard single
                                                                        format (A4) 

## Visa Fees
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants must not be asked to submit a physical passport photograph in cases where biometrics are captured (including digital photo enrolment) at the VAC.

## Processing Time
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 calendar days. This period may be extended
                                                                        up to a maximum of 60 calendar days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside
                                                                        the Schengen area and back again during your visit. If you apply for two or several entries, this must be justified. For more information on processing time please follow below link
                                                                    
Click here (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/processing_time.htm)

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS

After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you can walk into the Centre to submit your documents without an appointment between 0800hrs and 1200hrs. (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/login) Make sure to bring along all the required documents (#) and hand
                                                                        in supporting documents at the Visa Application  Centre (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/attend-centre/Nairobi).

visa_types.name: Online Application
visa_types.documents_required: Please click on the link Online Application
visa_types.visa_fees:
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
- VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19
visa_types.processing_time: Please refer to the Danish Immigration Service homepage  (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/)to obtain information about the processing time for an application of residence permit.
visa_types.forms: There are different applications forms dependent on which type of residence permit you apply for. You can see the list of forms on this link (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/NR/rdonlyres/AEBBEB05-947E-4B33-81E9-98D34CC12E48/0/us_form_guide_eng.pdf) and download the relevant form for your application.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A residence permit is given only upon approval by the Danish Immigration Service in Copenhagen – the process may take 4-6 months. Such a permit will be valid for Denmark only, not the entire Schengen-area. Application forms can be downloaded here. (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/NR/rdonlyres/AEBBEB05-947E-4B33-81E9-98D34CC12E48/0/us_form_guide_eng.pdf) (Please note that each of the Immigration Service’s application forms for Danish residence and work permits have been developed for a specific type of applicant.)
Further information can be found at the website of the Danish Immigration Service. (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A residence permit is given only upon approval by the Danish Immigration Service in Copenhagen – the process may take 4-6 months. Such a permit will be valid for Denmark only, not the entire Schengen-area. Application forms can be downloaded here. (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/NR/rdonlyres/AEBBEB05-947E-4B33-81E9-98D34CC12E48/0/us_form_guide_eng.pdf) (Please note that each of the Immigration Service’s application forms for Danish residence and work permits have been developed for a specific type of applicant.)
Further information can be found at the website of the Danish Immigration Service. (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/)

## Visa Fees
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
- VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19

## Documents Required

Please click on the link Online Application 

## Photo Specifications
Applicants must not be asked to submit a physical passport photograph in cases where biometrics are captured (including digital photo enrolment) at the VAC.

## Processing Time
Please refer to the Danish Immigration Service homepage  (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/)to obtain information about the processing time for an application of residence permit.

## Download Forms
There are different applications forms dependent on which type of residence permit you apply for. You can see the list of forms on this link (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/NR/rdonlyres/AEBBEB05-947E-4B33-81E9-98D34CC12E48/0/us_form_guide_eng.pdf) and download the relevant form for your application.

Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to travel to Denmark to visit family and friends, please follow these instructions.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to travel to Denmark to visit family and friends, please follow these instructions.

## Visa Fees
To find out about the visa fees please click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
- VFS service fee is KES 2865/EUR 19

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants must not be asked to submit a physical passport photograph in cases where biometrics are captured (including digital photo enrolment) at the VAC.

## Processing Time
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 calendar days. This period may be extended
                                                                        up to a maximum of 60 calendar days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside
                                                                        the Schengen area and back again during your visit. If you apply for two or several entries, this must be justified. For more information on processing time please Click here (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/processing_time.htm).

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa
                                                                        fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’
                                                                        .
                                                                    
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form 
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you can walk into the Centre to submit your documents without an appointment between 0800hrs and 1200hrs. You must book an appointment if you want to submit your documents between 1200hrs and 1500hrs at VFS by clicking here. (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/login) Make sure to bring along all the required documents (#) and
                                                                        hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application  Centre (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/dnk/attend-centre/Nairobi).
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
